author: @hardergamer
text: Lots of other things you can use like Nail varnish remover (Acetone) or even some plant foods with Nitrogen, but you need to understand what Nitrogen mixed with Iron or Steel or even Bleach can get very messy, also you can make your own Activated Carbon using charcoal and a chemical like Calcium Chloride, but Lemon juice will work with charcoal to make active carbon. Finally note: do not let aluminium near any of this that I have listed.

speaking of home made batteries I have a question for you.... I want to make a acid battery
that uses dissimilar metals, but I don't know which metals are the best to use, if I remember copper and zinc were the best voltage producers per cell I plan to make a large battery that will last a few years at a time that can power my 2000w inverter 12v input 120vac output
this may cost me quite a bit for the metal but I think it would be worth it to have power when the electricity goes out.  i figure 12"square plates per cell and enough plates to make 12 volts.... what do you suggest ? thanks in advance !

author: @Equelan2
text: I am still using crystal cell batteries which i made three months ago. They connected to a led bulb in my garden and that bulb glows every night automatically hanged to a tree. Increasing surface area will increase ampere and also increasing amount of cells connected in series will increase voltage. So having a 500w energy to run a fridge off grid at least is a matter of having materials required to producing battery cells. Hydrocloric acid is dangerous. People should avoid breathing in closed environments with that acid around becuse it can damage eyes and burn lung cells when breathing in the air contamined with HCI. I remmoned using sodium hydroxide as electrolye but in that case people should avoid using stainless steel. Because in time steel will react with alkaline and produce hexavalent chromium which is known as a carcinogenic matter. I am now sure if that is true but avoiding potantial harmfull materials is better for your safety.

author: @ianbutler1983
text:
Hi Robert,
My source for activated charcoal is dollar or pound store knock-off Brita-style water filter cartridges. They have quite a bit of it in them. The hydrochloric acid is sold here in the U.S. in hardware stores as "Muriatic Acid" with the masonry or with the paints and varnishes. Other than hardware stores it can often be bought in department stores in with the swimming pool supplies. 
I love the channel and your positive outlook is uplifting and inspiring. You give the unmistakable impression you are having great fun, succeed or fail.

author: @reypolice5231
text:
PROPOSED BUILD:

All Auto Mechanic shops have junk iron parts. Specifically the exhaust manifold.

All Mechanic shops have used batteries that the sell for the core. $1-10 Dollars.

Sell the ferric chloride or give it away to anyone that needs it like a machine shop.

If you have a golf course by you find out who replaced the deep cycle golf cart batteries. I by them for $15 each.

Reconditioned them as needed. They get replacement when they are only keeping half the charge.

Deep cycle golf cart batteries are better than car battery for storing power.

Trade your ferric chloride for brick cleaner or other with someone who needs it.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text: That's really usefull in an emergency. I'm not sure a phone will charge at 3V. USB spec defines 4.5V as a minimum and lithium ion needs 4.2V for the fixed voltage stage. Anyway, fun to try. I once showed my daughter the famous 2 coin battery with some salt water in between. She asked if it would replace an ordinairy battery. We calculated together how many we would need :-). Your's is a lot better.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: thanks for the pointer mate - it would need a couple more then - the thing is the actual cell voltage of a well made cell like this is around 0.6V - but made this way it has a high internal resistance - the better you make it the less you will need

author: @martinbonner8626
text: This could be where an Earth battery could be investigated . At a workshop I went to they suggested that biochar in  two trenches running parallel 500mm apart had a voltage difference of 1.5v when connected together . With biochar in the soil , iron in acidic soil and moisture . Biochar could be made with the help of your gasifier made a while ago to run a motor

replies.author: @reypolice5231
replies.text:
@ martin bonner

May I ask you to explain this build further?

I have seen earth battery builds before but never with the bio char as described.

replies.author: @martinbonner8626
replies.text:
@reypolice5231 , I’m not a soil scientist , but the way I understood it that two trenches eg 300mm wide , 300mm deep x 500mm apart , trenches filled with biochar , moist soil with an iron content (adding iron chelates , guessing) . Anodes and cathodes between was supposed to have a voltage of 1.5v . Could these be hooked up in series ? 

As I said I’m NOT a soil scientist ….. just the way I understand it ……. Something to do with Cation Exchange Capacity

replies.author: @reypolice5231
@reypolice5231
replies.text:
@martinbonner8626 

Also look up YT channel Patrick Kelly.

He goes over the 1835 patent in detail with links to website for full download of all his stuff.

What he showed was very well explained.

Website also very good.

This was used for telegraph lines in the US civil war covering many miles.

We never needed anything else.

Look for my other comment in this thread. I explain battery storage, And answer other people questions.

Hope it helps.

author: @jaypatel-yj9gw
text: This is really cool..... It would be nice if you make a video on converting different dc voltages to 5v so that one can charge phone or radio or something else from other type of batteries we find in every household... Because I think everyone has different types of batteries like 12v or 18v lithium ion from electric scooters or drills  and a car battery or maybe some AA type batteries.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice idea mate - cheers

replies.author: @reypolice5231
replies.text:
@ jay Patel

PROPOSED BUILD:

1. 5GAL PLASTIC BUCKET.
2. MAKE SEVERAL metal units in 5gal bucket, to meet your voltage DC. Several will fit. 

3. Connect all wires together to get your REQUIRE voltage.

4 have a lid on it to capture hydrogen for cooking, welding, or red balloon signal to your friends.

5. Build to 6 or 12 volts to run through a battery charger backwards to get alternating current at the plug end as needed.

6. Use this for generation connect to a car battery for storage.

7. Drain liquid as needed to stop reaction/ or remove the array ( metal parts) to stop reaction when car battery is fully charged.

replies.author: @jaypatel-yj9gw
replies.text: @reypolice5231 nice solution mate.... Though I was wondering, which one is better? One giant bucket or smaller buckets all connected?

replies.author: @reypolice5231
replies.text:
@jaypatel-yj9gw 

Further update:

The carbon should go in a sock or fabric sack to be easily compressed around a graphite rod. Why a carbon rod from an old battery would not work as well. Test to see.

Junk car exhaust manifold iron seems really available And free.

Mandatory all parts free, scavenged.

You will not be able to go to the store if it goes completely psycho. Collect all your supplies for multiple units. Starting now.

All your friends that you will depend on if things go sideways and they will will need your power build.

A friend in need is a friend in deed.

Have all your friends collect all parts. Build multiple units. Give your friends.

replies.author: @reypolice5231
replies.text:
@jaypatel-yj9gw

On a separate Matter

The people that get you parts and help you build, and answer your red balloon 🎈 signal are your  t r u e friends.

Find out who you Friends are.

author: @William_Hada
text: I love the idea of making this battery from household materials! Bravo! The brick cleaner version is similar to the Fe/Activated Carbon cells I have been building using FeCl3 electrolyte. I would not be surprised if the cell you built is actually rechargeable. I have been experimenting with building sealed Fe/Carbon coin type cells and recently tested one cell that sustained 101 charge/discharge cycles, at 0.84 volts. During this this 3 day testing period the output actually increased gradually 7%. I don't think my cell is an air battery because it was sealed with a silicone gasket.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you are probably making use of the Fe2+ /Fe3+ redox couple mate - here, basically the acid etches the iron - given H2 generation and consumption of the iron anode I wouldn't try to make this a sealed rechargeable version if I were you - but an Fe2+/Fe3+ version is more than doable for sure - if you try FeBr3 then you will get the Fe2+/Fe3+ couple on one side and Br2Br-/Br3- redox couple on the other side too

replies.author: @justinw1765
replies.text: "Air" is a bit of misnomer. What is really meant is oxygen and oxidation. You don't need air for that.  For example, if you're making Al air batteries, Oxi clean mixed in water works well to provide the oxygen necessary for the oxidation process.

author: @lightcapmath2777
text: Quick question...so would the iron wool be the Anode and the graphite be the Cathode or vise a versa?

replies.author: @SasquatchTrevor
replies.text: Iron would be the anode and carbon would be the cathode.

replies.author: @lightcapmath2777
replies.text:
@SasquatchTrevor Ok thanks, wanted to make sure...so the Fe oxidizes and the carbon reduces.. thanks again.
DVD

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good question mate - the iron is the anode - the cathode is a little more complicated - the carbon granules and graphite rod are actually the current collector and site for the reaction - the cathode is really the chlorine. The chlorine goes from Cl- (from the HCL) to Cl3- (now held in the FeCl3)

replies.author: @lightcapmath2777
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Understood many thanks for the clarification

author: @stevetobias4890
text:
Bloody brilliant Rob, a couple of questions. Would drain cleaner (diluted sulphuric acid) or phosphoric acid work? Also would crushing the activated carbon a little bit in a mortar and pestle help solidifying the carbon? Finally would this also work in a semi enclosed container with a couple of small holes in the top make much of a difference?
Also in a hurricane a lot of very small wind turbines would also be beneficial.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: HIya mate - there is a phenomenon called polarisation - this is where the surface of the metal gets covered in a film of material, salt or oxide - or even gas - which stops the acid coming into contact with the metal and therefore stops the reaction and so no generation will happen - phosphoric acid forms insoluble phosphates with iron so it would polarise quite quickly - sulphuric acid will work but more hydrogen is generated - about twice - so it would bubble and froth quit a bit - your idea of crushing the carbon is a good one - i wouldn't enclose this - it does generate hydrogen and the pressure would build up - you don't want this popping in your face mate

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering thank you for your excellent answer Rob, I didn't mean totally enclosed, this is why I asked about small holes but I can see that leaving it open would be best.
